title: ci failing on make vendor
body:
While testing [unrelated change](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4638/files) I got a CI `vendor` job failing that gave no indication about root cause. Running the same command seen on CI did not produce a failure locally.

```
make vendor && ./hack/tree_status.sh
```

Still, I was able to reproduce the error on another machine but again without any indication regarding what went wrong or how to effectively sort the issue:
```
tree is dirty, please sync the vendor.conf and commit all changes.

 M vendor/modules.txt
?? vendor/github.com/Microsoft/hcsshim/mksyscall_windows.go
?? vendor/github.com/containers/storage/pkg/archive/example_changes.go
```

comments.body:
On your host, try
make vendor-in-container

The problem is different versions of golang
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
